clinicalWorkupAsks.nextPhysicianVisit: Request the following labs together to cover multiple coaching questions:

required.reason: Monitor iron balance given Double Reds donation 2-3x/year + 65mg daily iron supplementation. Target 30-200 ng/mL. Below 30 = deficient despite supplement; above 200 = overloaded, reduce dose.
Complete iron workup alongside ferritin. Detects anemia or overload context.

recommended.reason: Baseline given current 5g/day psyllium (under the 10-15g dose needed for measurable LDL effect). If LDL elevated, bump psyllium to 10g/day for 8-12 weeks and recheck. Also relevant for fish oil dose review (currently 980mg EPA+DHA, sub-therapeutic for lipid effects).
One-time baseline. San Diego + outdoor play = likely sufficient, but a data point is cheap. Target 30-60 ng/mL.

recommendedFollowUpFromJuly2025Panel.reason: Hyperthyroidism is one of the most common drivers of elevated SHBG. Jul 2025 panel showed SHBG 83 (upper limit 50). Rule out thyroid driver before attributing high SHBG to genetic/idiopathic.
Liver synthesizes SHBG. Elevated enzymes or altered liver function can increase SHBG. Rule out liver-driven elevation.
Low-insulin states elevate SHBG. Given mixed metabolic picture, worth characterizing insulin sensitivity.

recommendedFollowUpFromJuly2025Panel.test: Repeat T panel in 3-6 months with free T + SHBG + Estradiol + Prolactin
recommendedFollowUpFromJuly2025Panel.reason: Confirm the elevated-SHBG pattern is persistent, not a one-time reading. Same collection protocol (morning, fasted, pre-exercise, not within 2-3 weeks of Power Red donation).

testosteroneHistory.notes: Reference range 40s: 264-916 ng/dL. Within normal. Collection time unknown - if drawn afternoon, AM equivalent would have been higher since T is ~50% lower PM vs AM.
Optimal morning collection. Above age-40 average (~500-600 ng/dL). Free T and SHBG not drawn - limited interpretation without them (see 2025-07 panel showing high-SHBG pattern).

testosteroneHistory.freeTestosteroneFlag: low-normal (at floor, 3.4 pg/mL above deficiency threshold)
testosteroneHistory.notes: KEY FINDING: elevated SHBG (83 vs upper limit 50) is pulling total T out of circulation. Total T 811 is upper-normal but free/bioavailable T are at the BOTTOM of range. This is a 'functionally low-T presentation' despite robust total T. Technically does not meet Endocrine Society 2018 hypogonadism criteria (free T above floor, no reported symptoms). Borderline high estradiol tracks with high total T (aromatization). Prolactin normal rules out pituitary cause. Next step: understand SHBG driver - thyroid (TSH + free T4), liver (AST/ALT/bilirubin), metabolic (fasting insulin / HbA1c). Source: Quest Diagnostics ZD478125F Men's Hormone Panel Expanded, ordering physician MARKS, Lianne, PWN Health.

nutritionGoals.primary: Steady fat loss preserving lean mass, strength, and game performance
nutritionGoals.rationale: Apr 20 body-comp overview showed +4.7 lbs fat / +1.9 lbs lean over 6 weeks. Initial 2-day/week deficit-at-BMR approach (set Apr 20 AM) was recalibrated later that day based on Helms 2014 and Aragon 2017 ISSN evidence: (1) rest-day target at BMR was more aggressive than needed, (2) intermittent restriction is not superior to daily small deficits per ISSN 2017, (3) weekly deficit was below the Helms 0.5%/wk floor anyway. Revised to moderate-deficit across 3 non-heavy-training days.

goals.name: Moderate-deficit days
goals.weeklyDeficitEstimate: ~900-2,100 cal/week = ~0.3-0.6 lb/week fat loss (within Helms 2014 0.5-1%/week lean-preserving range)
goals.rationale: Mon = post-Sunday-game recovery (no training). Thu = upper body lifting only (no lower-body demand). Sat = pre-Sunday-game prep (no training). Wed (heavy TB DL) and game days (Tue, Sun) stay at maintenance to fuel performance. Daily deficit of 300-500 cal below TDEE preserves lean mass per Helms 2014.
goals.evidence: Helms et al. 2014, JISSN (PMID 24864135): 0.5-1% BW/week loss rate for lean-mass preservation in resistance-trained athletes. Aragon et al. 2017 ISSN position stand (PMID 28630601): intermittent restriction not superior to daily small-deficit approach.

goals.name: Restaurant meal cap
goals.rationale: Hall 2019 ward study (Cell Metab) showed ultra-processed/restaurant food environments drive ~+500 cal/day spontaneous intake vs matched whole-food diets. Current pattern: 5-7 restaurant meals/week consistently drives 3,000+ cal days. Capping at 3 fixes the weekly caloric creep without daily macro tracking.

supersededGoals.name: Deficit rest days (original Apr 20 AM)
supersededGoals.supersededReason: Target was at BMR (~1,990 cal) which was more aggressive than needed. Math using rest-day TDEE (~2,400) showed actual deficit was only 300-400 cal per day = 600-800 cal/week, below the Helms 0.5%/week floor anyway. Revised to gentler 3-day approach.

dailySupplements.name: One A Day Men's multivitamin
dailySupplements.evidenceTier: Weak (athletes with adequate diet); see knowledge/supplements.md

dailySupplements.name: Fish oil
dailySupplements.evidenceTier: Moderate for athletes; dose may be sub-therapeutic for tendon/recovery effects (2-3g EPA+DHA typical)

dailySupplements.name: Iron + vitamin C
dailySupplements.evidenceTier: CLINICALLY APPROPRIATE for Double Reds donor (2-3x/year). Red Cross recommends 18-38mg elemental for 120 days post-Power Red; his ~940-1,410mg/year iron loss justifies chronic supplementation. Dose is slightly above official 18-38mg range but within typical OTC tablet strength. Annual ferritin check warranted to confirm iron balance.

dailySupplements.name: Magnesium glycinate
dailySupplements.evidenceTier: Moderate; appropriate dose and form for sleep/recovery

dailySupplements.name: Psyllium husk powder
dailySupplements.evidenceTier: Strong for cholesterol, moderate for glycemic control and satiety. Jovanovski 2018 meta-analysis (Am J Clin Nutr, PMID 30239559): psyllium reduces LDL cholesterol 5-10% at 7-15g/day. 5g/day is modest; could benefit from 2-3x daily dosing or bump to 10g for full LDL effect per FDA qualified health claim threshold.

dailySupplements.name: Momentous Collagen Peptides (1 scoop)
dailySupplements.dose: 15g collagen peptides (11.6g grass-fed bovine + 5.2g FORTIGEL) + 50mg vitamin C, in shake
dailySupplements.evidenceTier: Emerging for tendinopathy. Shaw 2017 (PMID 27852613) showed 15g gelatin/collagen + ~50mg vitamin C 30-60 min pre-loading doubles collagen synthesis biomarkers in serum. Praet 2019 (PMID 30609761) RCT used FORTIGEL specifically for achilles tendinopathy and found symptom improvements. Momentous formulation matches both protocols (15g + 50mg vit C, FORTIGEL inclusion). Optimal timing: 30-60 min PRE Wed/Fri HSR sessions for tendon-specific adaptation. Post-game/post-loading timing has weaker evidence but still contributes to general recovery.

activityLog.notes: Week 7 Thu Upper Body + Core. Clean triple-progression session on all BB compounds: Bench 140→145 (resets benchPress derived target, +10 lbs from Apr 7 re-baseline of 135), Lat Pulldown 140→145, Bent Over Row 140→145. Identical sets/reps to Apr 17 Week 6 session with +5 across the board — textbook double progression. DB OHP 30 lb DBs held at 3x8 (third pressing movement of session, fatigue-appropriate to hold). Core circuit RX'd. Recovery context: 8hr sleep (recovered from Apr 21→22 6hr disturbance), AM achilles 0-1/10 and during-session achilles 0/10 (Silbernagel pain-monitoring green). Pre-lift fuel: everything bagel + butter breakfast (590cal/85g carbs), shake without granola lunch (86g protein/590cal). No Clif bar pre-lift today. Week 7 upper volume: chest 4, back 8 (lat pulldown + row), rear delt/shoulder 6 (OHP + face pull), core 6 (pallof + woodchop) — all within Schoenfeld 6-10 set in-season range. Week 7 push continues per plan to skip Week 8 deload if fatigue signals stay green (Coleman 2024 reactive-deload framework).

Week 7 Fri lift SKIPPED in favor of 2 hours yardwork (per Apr 19 precedent: digging/weeding/standing and squatting = functional lower-body load, estimated 500-700 cal expenditure). Substitution is NOT equivalent to programmed Friday session: yardwork covers glute/quad/posterior chain via unloaded squat/hinge patterns but does NOT replicate the tendon-specific Beyer HSR loading. HSR cadence this week drops to 2x (Wed seated @150 only) vs Beyer 3x/week protocol. One week below target is not catastrophic for 12-week tendon adaptation timeline but is the piece that would benefit from make-up if feasible (Sat standing HSR is borderline ill-timed vs Sun basketball per Silbernagel; alternatively, accept 2x this week and resume 3x in Week 8). Goblet squat, hip thrust, lateral band walk, single-leg balance, and banded ankle dorsiflexion also missed — minor impact, these are maintenance work. Timing vs Sunday basketball: yardwork load plus no formal lift leaves ~36+ hrs pre-game, similar readiness profile to standard Fri lift.

Week 8 Day 1 - Sunday AM basketball, 40 min high intensity. Estimated 400-500 cal expenditure. Sleep night Apr 25→26 'good' (~8hr) - second consecutive recovery night. AM achilles 0-1/10, during/after game 0-1/10 (Silbernagel green continues). Pre-game fuel: Apr 25 dinner provided ~146g carbs (2 russets + ItsIt) for glycogen, plus AM shake post-game ~60g carbs. Post-game: shake without granola with yogurt + 1 scoop Momentous Collagen Peptides (15g + 50mg vit C, FORTIGEL formulation - exact Shaw 2017/Praet 2019 protocol match). Timing was post-game (post-loading) - weaker evidence than 30-60 min pre-loading; first practice of optimal pre-loading timing comes Tue Apr 28 bonus HSR session. No hockey PM this week.

exercises.weight: ~287 lbs effective (220 lb BW + 67 lb machine starting load), single-leg
exercises.notes: Standing calf raise machine, single-leg — body weight loads through the working calf PLUS 67 lbs of machine starting resistance. Effective per-leg load ≈ 220 BW + 67 machine = ~287 lbs. Note: not directly comparable to Phase 1 seated bilateral (150 lbs total / 75 lbs/leg) — seated bilateral was soleus-bias (knee bent, BW does not load the calves), Phase 2 standing single-leg is gastroc-bias (knee straight, BW loads through the calf). Different muscle group emphasis per Beyer 2015 protocol calling for both variants. At week 9 of HSR programming the protocol calls for 6RM zone; 3x6 with 3s/3s tempo at this load lands cleanly in that zone. 1st of 2 HSR sessions this week (no Tue HSR — Tue was a game; Fri standing HSR caps the week at 2/3, accepted in Wk 9 plan).

scienceReviews.findings: STRENGTH: Cleanest 2-week progression block of Phase 1. Week 7 Wed: TB DL stall broken (5x5 @ 230 completed after 5-week plateau, volume-before-intensity strategy from Week 6 review delivered). Back Squat 140→145 RPE 7 (Phase 1 target hit). Week 7 Thu: triple-progression on all BB compounds — Bench 140→145, Lat Pulldown 140→145, Bent Row 140→145 (textbook double progression after 2 consecutive sessions at 140). DB OHP held at 30 lb DBs (third pressing of session, fatigue-appropriate). Net: 4 of 7 main lifts +5 in Week 7. ACHILLES: 7-day all-green streak — every reading 0-1/10 across training, games, yardwork, AND 2 yellow sleep nights. Tendon resilience under fatigue is the strongest possible signal that HSR protocol is working (Silbernagel framework). HSR CADENCE: Week 7 hit 1/3 sessions only (Wed seated @150 completed, Fri standing skipped for 2hr yardwork). Below Beyer 3x/week target — single-week shortfall, not catastrophic for 12-week adaptation timeline but the structural issue worth addressing. SLEEP: Week 7 mixed pattern — 2 nights 6hr-with-disturbance (Apr 21→22, Apr 23→24), 2 recovery nights (Apr 22→23, Apr 24→25). 2/4 yellow, 2/4 green. Not a Section 2 reactive-deload trigger (requires 3+ consecutive <6hr) but trending. Week 8 entered with 2 consecutive recovery nights (Apr 24→25, Apr 25→26 both 'good'). NUTRITION: Apr 20-26 protein avg ~175g/day (above 160g floor); Apr 25 was lowest day at 116g (below floor by 44g — driver was egg-based dinner instead of chicken-based, single-decision miss). Calorie pattern Apr 20-25 raw avg 2,698 cal/day with Fri 3,417 spike from Texas Roadhouse (offset by yardwork ~600 cal). Net of activity: ~2,500-2,600 cal/day = at maintenance, not deficit. Saturated fat NEW concern: Thu burger (~30g) + Fri ribeye + apps (~50g) + Sat ribs/eggs/bacon (~38g) = ~120g sat fat across 3 days, well over AHA 22g/day ceiling. 4-week pattern matters for cardiovascular markers given longevity goal. BODY COMP: 221.6-222.4 lbs / 23.3-23.8% BF across the window, slow recomp continuing (Apr 8 vs Apr 23: 222.4/23.6% → 222.0/23.4%). VOLUME: Week 7 lower body 14 sets (TB DL 5 + Squat 3 + BSS 3 + SLRDL 3), upper 14 sets (Bench 4 + Lat 4 + Row 4 + OHP 3 + face pull 3 — Bench at floor of 6-10 range). Calf HSR 3 sets only (Wed only) vs target 9-12 (3 sessions x 3 sets). NEW PROTOCOL: User adopted Momentous Collagen Peptides (15g + 50mg vit C, FORTIGEL formulation) as permanent shake default Apr 26 — exact match to Shaw 2017 (PMID 27852613) and Praet 2019 (PMID 30609761) achilles tendinopathy protocols. Initial timing post-game; better-leverage timing is 30-60 min PRE Wed/Fri HSR sessions. PHASE TRANSITION: 1 week from Phase 1→2 (Week 9 start May 3). scienceReviews.findings:
Phase 1 → Phase 2 transition checkpoint (Section 7).

STRENGTH (end of Phase 1): TB DL closes Phase 1 at 235x4x5 (+5 lb microload Apr 29 broke a 5-week 230 plateau — strongest single result of Phase 1). Back Squat 150x3x5, hit Phase 1 target. BB Bench 145x4x5. Vs 12-month targets: TB DL needs +150 / +64% to 385x5; Back Squat needs +165 / +110% to 315x5; Bench needs +80 / +55% to 225x5. Section 7 says wait 2-3 weeks into Phase 2 before revising — hold all targets, re-evaluate at Wk 11.

HSR COMPLIANCE: Week 8 hit Beyer 3x/week target for the first time in Phase 1 (Tue standing + Wed seated + Fri standing). Wk 6: 2/3, Wk 7: 1/3. The structural fix was the bonus Tuesday session — pattern worth carrying into Phase 2 weeks where Tue isn't a game day.

ACHILLES: 13-day all-green streak Apr 22 → May 1 across training, games, yardwork, sleep-poor nights, and a TB DL plateau-breaker. Strongest signal of the program that the Beyer 2015 HSR protocol is delivering. Sun May 3 game: not reported.

SLEEP: Apr 26 → Apr 30 hit 6 consecutive recovery nights (8h reported). Apr 30 → May 1 broke streak at 5.5h (single night, below the Section 2 reactive-deload trigger of 3+ consecutive). Friday plan correctly applied Knowles 2018 partial deload — volume -25%, intensity held, HSR full load.

VOLUME (Wk 8): Lower body 17-19 hard sets across Tue + Wed + Fri (mid-range of Section 3 6-10 sets/muscle/wk after sport contribution). Upper ~13 sets (in range). Calves 9 sets across 3 HSR sessions = on Beyer target.

Resolves naturally at Phase 2: existing Phase 2 plan drops BSS from Wed and moves it to Fri at moderate load. No custom rule needed.

BODY COMP: 221.3 lbs / 23.2% BF on Apr 28 — best reading of program (-1.1 lb / -0.4 pt BF over 20 days). Slow recomp continuing on Option B deficit-anchor protocol.

SAT FAT (continuing concern, longevity lens): Apr 26 → May 1 ran 5/6 days at or over the AHA 22 g/day ceiling (Apr 26 ~36g, Apr 28 ~22.5g, Apr 29 ~31g, Apr 30 ~41g, May 1 ~20.5g+ pre-dinner). PHASE 2 READINESS: All criteria met — strength progressing, achilles tolerant, HSR compliant, sport recovery good. Plyo introduction appropriate. Section 6 prescribes 40-60 contacts/session for non-specialized athletes; the existing Phase 2 Fri default lands at 30 contacts (Box Jump 3x4 + TB Jump Squat 3x3 + Broad Jump 3x3) — deliberately below floor for Wk 1 first plyo session in 8 weeks given bilateral achilles tendinopathy. scienceReviews.findings:
Wk 11 / Wk 3 of Phase 2 review (13 days since last review on 2026-05-04 — overdue by 6 days vs weekly cadence target).

STRENGTH PROGRESSION (Section 7 checkpoint — Wk 11 was the planned re-evaluation point):
- TB DL: 225 → 240 lb over 8 wks = 1.88 lb/wk = 8.1 lb/month. Inside Section 7 expected range (5-15 lb/month for months 0-6, intermediate). 145 lb remaining to target. At current pace, 385x5 lands ~18 months out; 12-month timeline plausible only if Phase 2 RPE 7-8 work accelerates from Phase 1 RPE 6-7.
- Back Squat: rebuilt from 45 lb pattern-work (Apr 8) to 155x4x5 (May 6). Real progression now ~5 lb/cycle. 160 lb to target. CLAUDE.md called 15-18 months more realistic than 12 for squat — current data supports that.
- Bench Press: 135 → 150 in 30 days = 3.5 lb/wk = 15.2 lb/month. Top of Section 7 range. 75 lb to target. Most realistic of the three; ~5-6 months at current pace.

HSR COMPLIANCE (the biggest miss this review):
- Wk 9: 2/3 sessions (Wed seated + Fri standing single-leg). Bonus Tue dropped vs Wk 8.
- Wk 10: 1/3 sessions (Fri only — Wed/Thu missed to work travel).
- Wk 11 to date: 0/3.
- 3 consecutive weeks below Beyer 2015 (PMID 26018970, Strong) protocol target. Wk 8 success was the bonus Tue HSR session; that pattern wasn't carried into Phase 2. Outcomes are protective so far (see Achilles), but compliance is the lagging indicator; Magnusson and Kjaer 2019 (PMC6395417, Strong) on multi-month tendon adaptation cycles argues against extended compliance gaps.

ACHILLES (the strongest signal in the program):
- 15-event green-zone (≤3/10) streak back to 2026-03-17. Zero events in yellow (4-5) or red (>5) range across past 30 days.
- Plyo introduction (5/9, 30 contacts) and ramp (5/15, 40 contacts) both completed at 0/10.
- Two plyo→game 48h clusters (Sat 5/9 → Sun 5/10; Fri 5/15 → Sun 5/17) both clean.
- Meets Section 4 criteria to continue Fri plyo block by wide margin.

PLYO BLOCK (Section 6):
- On schedule per 5/04 review forecast. Wk 9: 30 contacts (ramp floor). Wk 10: 40 contacts (+10 ramp, hitting Section 6 floor). Wk 11 prescribed 50, Wk 12 prescribed 60 (top of 40-60 range). After Wk 12 close, 2-week maintenance Wks 13-14 per Phase 3 plan.
- Markovic 2007 (Strong): ~4-8% VJ improvement from 6-12 weeks of plyo in trained athletes. The 4-week block targets the steeper portion of that curve.

VOLUME (Wk 10 anomaly):
- Wk 10 = 1 training session (Fri plyo) vs typical 3, due to work travel. Net: zero direct upper-body sets, zero non-plyo lower-body sets.
- Functionally an unscheduled reactive 'deload.' Per Coleman 2024 (PMID 38274324, Moderate), unscheduled deloads without fatigue signals can cost strength gain — but Wk 10 was sport/work-driven not fatigue-driven, and adjacent outcomes (achilles, game performance) were intact. Treat as one-off, not a pattern.

RECOVERY DATA GAP:
- Sleep logged 4/14 nights (29%) since last review.
- Protein logged 3/14 days (21%).
- Body comp: 1 reading (5/7: 220.5 lb / 23.0% BF, essentially flat from 5/04 baseline of 221.3 / 23.2%).
- Per CLAUDE.md: 'Data gaps on these two fields compromise the 12-week HSR adaptation check and the Knowles 2018 sleep-intensity auto-regulation.' Now a compounded gap across two consecutive reviews (Wk 6 review also flagged this).

PHASE 2 STATUS:
- Wk 11 = Wk 3 of Phase 2. RPE targets shifted from 6-7 (Phase 1) to 7-8 (Phase 2) — too early to tell if working-set RPE actually moved; the Wk 11 + Wk 13 sessions will be the read.
- 4 weeks until Phase 3 entry (Wk 15, mid-June). Phase 2 plyo block runs Wks 9-12; maintenance Wks 13-14; Phase 3 begins 'power realization' work.
scienceReviews.changes:
1) HSR COMPLIANCE FIX (highest-leverage adjustment this review): Adopt a default Mon-AM HSR-only mini-session (10-15 min: 3 sets seated + 3 sets standing single-leg, full Beyer protocol load and tempo). Travel-proof (no equipment dependency beyond a step + DBs), low-CNS-cost before Tue basketball, and protects Beyer 3x/wk compliance even when Wed/Thu fall out. Pattern modeled on the Wk 8 bonus-Tue success. References: Beyer 2015 (PMID 26018970, Strong); Magnusson and Kjaer 2019 (PMC6395417, Strong) on tendon load continuity.

2) RECOVERY CAPTURE TRIGGER: Standardize a single morning log: sleep hours / AM achilles 0-10 / protein floor estimate (Y/N hit 40g by lunch). Target 80% capture (≥5/7 days/week). The Silbernagel 24-hour rule and Knowles 2018 sleep-intensity auto-regulation both depend on consistent daily reads. References: Silbernagel 2007 (PMID 17307888, Strong); Knowles 2018 (PMID 29422383, Moderate); Walsh 2021 IOC consensus (PMID 33144349, Strong).

3) HOLD 12-MONTH STRENGTH TARGETS THROUGH WK 14: Re-evaluate after Phase 2 plyo block closes (5/29) and 2-week maintenance Wks 13-14 complete. Current progression rates (TB DL 8.1 lb/mo, Bench 15.2 lb/mo) are inside Section 7 expected ranges; Back Squat will be the constraint and likely confirms the 15-18 month timeline CLAUDE.md flagged. Hold for now. References: Rhea 2003 dose-response meta-analysis (Moderate); Baker 2001 (PMID 11710401, Moderate) on in-season constraint.

WK 11 FORECAST: Mon 5/18 HSR mini-session (the new default). Tue 5/19 basketball. Wed 5/20 Phase 2 Lower (first heavy TB DL touch in 14 days — hold 240 lb for one session, expect small RPE bump from layoff, push to 245 only after clean rep quality per Section 1 progression-stall protocol). Thu 5/21 Phase 2 Upper + Power. Fri 5/22 Phase 2 Power + Plyo at 50 contacts (5x4 / 5x3 / 5x3 or 4x4 / 4x4 / 4x4 — pick the cleaner split). Sat 5/23 rest. Sun 5/24 basketball. Next science review: Sun 5/24 (end of Wk 11), then weekly cadence.

scienceReviews.findings:
Wk 12 end-of-week / plyo-block-close review (13 days since the 2026-05-17 review; the 5/17 entry named today's checkpoint - re-evaluate Section 7 targets after the block closes 5/29). Window covered: 5/18 (Wk 11 back-half) through 5/29 (Wk 12 close).

STRENGTH (Section 7 re-evaluation - the purpose of this checkpoint):
- TB DL: held 240 for a 2nd clean session 5/20 (a 14-day layoff did NOT produce the forecast RPE bump - pattern intact), then +5 to 245 on 5/27 (clean, first compound +5 in 4 weeks). Now 140 lb from the 385x5 target. Rate is still ~8 lb/month (Wk 11 review figure); the Phase 2 RPE 7-8 shift has not yet accelerated TB DL beyond its Phase 1 rate. At this pace 385x5 lands ~17-18 months out, NOT 12. Honest read: TB DL 385 is an 18-month target.
- Bench: held 150 for a 2nd clean session 5/21, then +5 to 155 on 5/28 (clean, RPE 7-8). Now 70 lb from 225x5 at ~15 lb/month (Wk 11 figure) = ~4-5 months. On pace for the 12-month target and arguably ahead. Most reliable of the three.
- Back Squat: held 155 (5/20, 2nd clean session, opened 160) but the 160 push was DEFERRED on 5/27 - no squat rack available, substituted Leg Press 3x10 (90/180/270). So an earned +5 is un-cashed and the constraint lift lost a progression to equipment, not to the athlete. 160 lb from 315x5; CLAUDE.md's 15-18 month call is confirmed by the data. Equipment availability is now a live risk for the squat specifically.
Section 7 conclusion: Bench 225 holds at 12 months (tracking ahead); TB DL 385 and Squat 315 are honestly 18-month targets at observed rates. Recommend re-horizoning rather than carrying targets the data won't support (Rhea 2003 dose-response, Moderate; Baker 2001 PMID 11710401 in-season constraint; Lexell 1995 age recovery factor).

HSR COMPLIANCE (Beyer 2015 PMID 26018970, Strong - the headline reversal):
- Wk 11: 3/3 (Wed seated + Wed single-leg + Fri single-leg). First 3/3 since Wk 8.
- Wk 12: 3/3 (Wed single-leg + Fri seated + Fri single-leg double-variant make-up).
- TWO consecutive Beyer-compliant weeks, reversing the Wk 9-11 decline (2/3 -> 1/3 -> 0/3-at-last-review). The 5/17 structural fix (restore Seated Calf to Wed) is the direct driver.
- CAVEAT: both weeks were recovered by reactive make-up, not the prescribed Mon-AM HSR mini-session. The Monday anchor (the 5/17 review's #1 fix) was NOT adopted either week (5/18 = beach walk instead, 5/25 = rest). Friday double-up works but is fragile - one forgotten Friday = a missed week. Durability still depends on the unrealized Monday anchor. Process note: the 5/17 Seated-Calf fix was added to modifiedWeeks and the activityLog but never to the base index.html prescription, which is why Seated was missed Wed 5/27 (patched 5/27). Fixes must land in the base prescription, not just the modified week.

VOLUME (Section 3, 6-10 hard sets/muscle/wk + sport): Both weeks in range. Wk 11 lower ~14 sets + plyo + 2 games, upper ~15 sets, calves 9 (on Beyer target), core at floor. Wk 12 lower ~13 sets (Leg Press preserved quad volume when the rack was unavailable), upper ~15 sets, calves 9. No volume concern.

ACHILLES (Silbernagel 2007 PMID 17307888, Strong): Green zone (<=3/10) unbroken back to 3/17 - now ~10+ weeks. Only nonzero in-window was 5/27 AM 1/10 (resolved with warmup, still green, no action per Section 4). The tendon tolerated the FULL plyo ramp 30 -> 60 contacts without leaving green - strongest signal in the program that the Beyer HSR protocol is working. GAP: the 5/29 post-plyo achilles read (the single most informative point of the block - highest-load session of the week) is not yet reported.

RECOVERY / NUTRITION (Section 5):
- Sleep: IMPROVED this window - captured on 5/17, 5/20, 5/21, 5/22, 5/24, 5/26, 5/27, 5/28 (~70%, up from 29% at the Wk 11 review). No sub-6h nights; 5/27 was 7h (low-normal, NSF range, not a Section 2 trigger). The recovery-capture trigger is partially landing for sleep.
- Protein: 0% captured. bodyLog has ZERO protein entries 5/18-5/30 (only a 5/28 weight/BF reading). Worse than the 21% at the Wk 11 review. This is the binding constraint: every nutrition and recomp conclusion below is un-diagnosable without intake data (Morton 2018 PMID 28698222 distribution; the daily protein-floor read is the missing field).
- Body comp: 5/28 = 220.5 lb / 23.0% BF, IDENTICAL to 5/7. Flat at program-best for 3 weeks - holding, not regressing, but the slow-recomp has plateaued. Cannot diagnose the plateau (intake unlogged).

PLYO BLOCK CLOSE (Section 6 / Markovic 2007, Strong; Ebben 2010): Full 4-week block complete - 30 (5/9) -> 40 (5/15) -> 50 (5/22) -> 60 (5/29), landing at the top of the 40-60 range, every session clean achilles. Markovic 2007 projects ~4-8% VJ gain over 6-12 wk; this 4-wk block targets the steep early portion. MEASUREMENT GAP: VJ is goal #3 and the plyo block is its primary driver, but there is NO pre/post vertical-jump test in the data - the block closed with no way to quantify whether it moved VJ. Wks 13-14 = 2-week plyo maintenance per the Phase 3 plan; Phase 3 (power realization) starts Wk 15 (~Jun 14).

DELOAD: Wk 12 is a scheduled every-4th-week deload (4/8/12). Correctly SKIPPED (ran normally with 2 progressions landed) - skip-criteria all green (lifts progressing, RPE on target, achilles stable, no sub-6h sleep), consistent with Coleman 2024 PMID 38274324 (prophylactic deloads in non-fatigued lifters cost gains). Continues the Wk 8 skip pattern. Next scheduled deload Wk 16, by which point Phase 3 is underway.

PHASE 2 -> 3 READINESS: Strong. Two +5 progressions (TB DL, Bench) landed at RPE 7-8 in Wk 12, so the Phase 2 intensity bump is tolerated and productive. Maintenance Wks 13-14 then Phase 3 is the right sequence (Section 6 diminishing returns after a 4-wk block).
scienceReviews.changes:
1) PROTEIN/INTAKE LOGGING IS THE #1 FIX (it gates every nutrition + recomp conclusion). 0% protein capture this window and body comp flat 3 weeks at program-best - the plateau is un-diagnosable without intake. Not more analysis; capture. Prioritize the single daily protein-floor read (hit 40g by lunch Y/N) above all other fields since that is the 0% gap. Target >=5/7 days. References: Morton 2018 (PMID 28698222, Strong); Schoenfeld and Aragon 2018.

2) SECTION 7 TARGET RE-HORIZON (recommendation, pending athlete sign-off - not yet written to data.targets): hold Bench 225 at 12 months (tracking ahead, ~4-5 mo); re-horizon TB DL 385 and Squat 315 to ~18 months, which the observed rates (TB DL ~8 lb/mo, Squat the in-season constraint) actually support. Also: cash the un-cashed Back Squat 160 in Wk 13 IF a rack is available - the constraint lift should not keep losing earned progressions to equipment. References: Rhea 2003 (Moderate); Baker 2001 (PMID 11710401, Moderate); Lexell 1995.

3) INSTALL THE MONDAY HSR ANCHOR DURING WKS 13-14 (durability fix): two 3/3 weeks is real, but both were fragile Friday catch-ups, not the prescribed Mon-AM mini-session. Wks 13-14 are plyo maintenance (lower lift volume) - the low-cost window to actually build the Monday HSR mini-session (3 sets seated + 3 sets standing single-leg, full Beyer load/tempo) into habit so compliance survives a dropped Wed/Fri. Also confirm the Seated Calf fix is now in the base index.html prescription, not just modified weeks. References: Beyer 2015 (PMID 26018970, Strong); Magnusson and Kjaer 2019 (PMC6395417, Strong) tendon load continuity.

WORTH DOING (not a core adjustment): establish a vertical-jump baseline (jump-reach minus standing-reach, or a phone jump-test) before Wks 13-14 so the next plyo block can be evaluated against goal #3. The block that just closed has no VJ measurement (Markovic 2007, Strong).

OPEN DATA ITEM: the 5/29 post-plyo achilles read and Thu/Fri-night sleep are still unreported - needed to close the block record (Silbernagel 24-hour rule).

NEXT REVIEW: Sun 6/7 (end of Wk 13), weekly cadence.

STRUCTURAL HSR FIX: discontinue the standalone Monday anchor (missed 5/18, 5/25, 6/8) and bolt HSR onto the lift days that actually happen - Wed Upper + Thu Lower + Fri Plyo each carry HSR = 3/3 by riding committed sessions (Beyer 2015 PMID 26018970, Strong; habit needs a trigger). WED PRIORITY = UPPER: barbell pressing dark since 5/28 (13 days), chest/back below the Schoenfeld 2017 6-set weekly minimum; Bench is the only lift on its 12-month pace (~15 lb/mo toward 225x5), so recover it first. Hold 155, +5 to 160 only if crisp. VJ baseline goes FIRST on Wed (freshest legs: Mon rest + only light games since) - goal #3 has no measurement and the just-closed plyo block can't be evaluated without one (Markovic 2007, Strong). THU LOWER = maintenance hold (TB DL 245, Squat 160, RPE 6-7, no PR) - keep the pattern without overreaching before a power phase (Baker 2001 PMID 11710401, Moderate). FRI PLYO = ~30-contact maintenance dose, half the 60-contact block peak (Ebben 2010; Markovic 2007), 48h before the Sun game (the proven plyo->game cluster, all clean). Sat rest absorbs the Wed/Thu/Fri load. STANDING GAPS unchanged: protein 0% logged since 5/11 (the binding constraint on every body-comp read), achilles + sleep capture still the ask. Strength targets per the Wk 12 re-horizon: Bench 225 at 12 months (on pace), TB DL 385 / Squat 315 at ~18 months.
